Commercial Stucco in Bell County, TX

Bell County's commercial landscape — anchored by Fort Cavazos, a growing healthcare sector in Temple, and expanding retail in Killeen — requires stucco contractors who can handle projects of every scale. Star Stucco of Austin brings commercial-grade expertise to properties throughout the county.

Get a Free EstimateCall (512) 706-9699

Serving Bell County's Diverse Commercial Market

Bell County sits at the northern edge of Central Texas, where the Blackland Prairie meets the limestone hill country. The mix of military-driven retail near Fort Cavazos, medical office development in Temple, and downtown revitalization efforts in Belton creates a wide range of commercial stucco needs that our team is well-equipped to handle.

Commercial properties in Bell County face the same punishing Central Texas summers as the Austin area, with temperatures frequently exceeding 100 degrees and intense afternoon thunderstorms testing exterior wall systems. Our material selections and application methods are calibrated for these conditions, producing stucco finishes that resist cracking and fading over the long term.

Whether you manage a strip mall along Highway 190, an office building near Scott & White Medical Center, or a restaurant on Belton's historic square, our team brings the same level of professionalism and attention to detail that has built our reputation across the region.

What's Included With Our Commercial Stucco in Bell County

Every project includes expert craftsmanship, quality materials, and a commitment to lasting results.

Site Assessment

We evaluate existing wall conditions, drainage patterns, and building access at your Bell County commercial property before developing a work plan.

Project Planning & Scheduling

Our schedule accounts for your business operations, local weather patterns, and any coordination needed with other trades working on the building.

Surface Preparation

Existing surfaces are cleaned, loose material removed, and appropriate bonding agents applied to create a solid foundation for the new stucco system.

Commercial-Grade Application

Base and brown coats are applied with consistent thickness across all wall areas, using commercial mixes designed for the demands of large-scale exterior surfaces.

Finish Coat & Detailing

The final texture and color are applied to match your building's architectural style, with clean transitions at openings, joints, and decorative elements.

Final Inspection & Warranty

We walk the completed project with you to confirm quality, address any touch-ups, and provide warranty documentation for your records.

Common Commercial Stucco Needs in Bell County

Bell County commercial properties span everything from military-adjacent retail to downtown office space. These are the stucco projects we see most often in the area.

New Construction Exteriors

New retail pads, medical offices, and mixed-use buildings going up along the I-35 corridor between Temple and Belton need stucco exteriors that are built right from the start.

Storefront Renovation

Older commercial strips near Fort Cavazos and along Highway 190 in Killeen are being updated with fresh stucco to attract new tenants and improve property values.

Multifamily Buildings

Apartment complexes serving the military community and Temple's healthcare workforce need exterior stucco that holds up under high occupancy and heavy use.

Office Park Stucco

Professional office clusters near Baylor Scott & White and along Adams Avenue in Temple maintain polished exteriors with stucco that projects stability and professionalism.

Retail Center Restoration

Aging shopping centers in Harker Heights and Killeen often need stucco patching, recoating, or full restoration after years of sun exposure and storm damage.

HOA Common Areas

Community amenity buildings, entry walls, and clubhouses across Bell County developments require stucco repair and recoating that matches the original neighborhood design.

Why Choose Us for Commercial Stucco in Bell County?

  • Experience with commercial projects across Temple, Belton, Killeen, and Harker Heights
  • Insurance and bonding that meet requirements for military-adjacent and institutional projects
  • Scheduling that respects retail operating hours and minimizes lost revenue for tenant businesses
  • Dust and debris containment practices that keep neighboring storefronts and walkways clean
  • Knowledge of Bell County municipal codes and inspection processes for commercial construction
  • Durable material systems selected for the hot, storm-prone climate of Central Texas

Licensed & Insured

Full liability coverage and workers' compensation for every project in Bell County.

20+ Years Experience

Decades of hands-on stucco work across Central Texas residential and commercial properties.

Certified Applicators

Certified by Sto Corp, Senergy/BASF, Master Wall, Dryvit, and Parex LaHabra for the highest quality results.

Our Commercial Stucco Process

A straightforward approach from start to finish — no surprises, no shortcuts.

1

Schedule a Commercial Consultation

Call us to arrange a property visit at your Bell County commercial site. We will review the project scope and discuss your timeline and budget.

2

Site Inspection & Scope

We document wall conditions, take accurate measurements, and note any access challenges so the proposal reflects the real scope of work.

3

Detailed Bid & Timeline

You get a straightforward proposal with itemized costs, material details, and a realistic project calendar you can share with tenants or building owners.

4

Professional Execution

Our crews arrive on schedule, maintain a clean site, and deliver the finished stucco project according to the agreed specifications and timeline.
